function.name: mcp_brave_search_brave_local_search
function.description:
Brave Local Search API provides enrichments for location search results. Access to this API is available only through the Brave Search API Pro plans; confirm the user's plan before using this tool (if the user does not have a Pro plan, use the brave_web_search tool). Searches for local businesses and places using Brave's Local Search API. Best for queries related to physical locations, businesses, restaurants, services, etc.
    
    Returns detailed information including:
        - Business names and addresses
        - Ratings and review counts
        - Phone numbers and opening hours

    Use this when the query implies 'near me', 'in my area', or mentions specific locations (e.g., 'in San Francisco'). This tool automatically falls back to brave_web_search if no local results are found.

country.description: Search query country, where the results come from. The country string is limited to 2 character country codes of supported countries.

search_lang.description: Search language preference. The 2 or more character language code for which the search results are provided.

ui_lang.description: The language of the UI. The 2 or more character language code for which the search results are provided.

count.description: Number of results (1-20, default 10). Applies only to web search results (i.e., has no effect on locations, news, videos, etc.)

safesearch.description: Filters search results for adult content. The following values are supported: 'off' - No filtering. 'moderate' - Filters explicit content (e.g., images and videos), but allows adult domains in search results. 'strict' - Drops all adult content from search results. The default value is 'moderate'.

freshness.description: Filters search results by when they were discovered. The following values are supported: 'pd' - Discovered within the last 24 hours. 'pw' - Discovered within the last 7 days. 'pm' - Discovered within the last 31 days. 'py' - Discovered within the last 365 days. 'YYYY-MM-DDtoYYYY-MM-DD' - Timeframe is also supported by specifying the date range e.g. [REDACTED_PHONE]to[REDACTED_PHONE].

text_decorations.description: Whether display strings (e.g. result snippets) should include decoration markers (e.g. highlighting characters).

spellcheck.description: Whether to spellcheck the provided query.

function.name: mcp_brave_search_brave_news_search
function.description:
This tool searches for news articles using Brave's News Search API based on the user's query. Use it when you need current news information, breaking news updates, or articles about specific topics, events, or entities.
    
    When to use:
        - Finding recent news articles on specific topics
        - Getting breaking news updates
        - Researching current events or trending stories
        - Gathering news sources and headlines for analysis

    Returns a JSON list of news-related results with title, url, and description. Some results may contain snippets of text from the article.
    
    When relaying results in markdown-supporting environments, always cite sources with hyperlinks.
    
    Examples:
        - "According to [Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/technology/china-bans/), China bans uncertified and recalled power banks on planes".
        - "The [New York Times](https://www.nytimes.com/2025/06/27/us/technology/ev-sales.html) reports that Tesla's EV sales have increased by 20%".
        - "According to [BBC News](https://www.bbc.com/news/world-europe-65910000), the UK government has announced a new policy to support renewable energy".

function.name: mcp_brave_search_brave_summarizer
function.description:
Retrieves AI-generated summaries of web search results using Brave's Summarizer API. This tool processes search results to create concise, coherent summaries of information gathered from multiple sources.

    When to use:

    - When you need a concise overview of complex topics from multiple sources
    - For quick fact-checking or getting key points without reading full articles
    - When providing users with summarized information that synthesizes various perspectives
    - For research tasks requiring distilled information from web searches

    Returns a text summary that consolidates information from the search results. Optional features include inline references to source URLs and additional entity information.

    Requirements: Must first perform a web search using brave_web_search with summary=true parameter. Requires a Pro AI subscription to access the summarizer functionality.

key.description: The key is equal to value of field key as part of the Summarizer response model.

entity_info.description: Returns extra entities info with the summary response.

inline_references.description: Adds inline references to the summary response.

function.name: mcp_context7_query_docs
function.description:
Retrieves and queries up-to-date documentation and code examples from Context7 for any programming library or framework.

You must call 'Resolve Context7 Library ID' tool first to obtain the exact Context7-compatible library ID required to use this tool, UNLESS the user explicitly provides a library ID in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version' in their query.

Do not call this tool more than 3 times per question.

libraryId.description: Exact Context7-compatible library ID (e.g., '/mongodb/docs', '/vercel/next.js', '/supabase/supabase', '/vercel/next.js/v14.3.0-canary.87') retrieved from 'resolve-library-id' or directly from user query in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version'.

query.description: The question or task you need help with. Be specific and include relevant details. Good: 'How to set up authentication with JWT in Express.js' or 'React useEffect cleanup function examples'. Bad: 'auth' or 'hooks'. The query is sent to the Context7 API for processing. Do not include any sensitive or confidential information such as API keys, passwords, credentials, personal data, or proprietary code in your query.

function.name: mcp_context7_resolve_library_id
function.description:
Resolves a package/product name to a Context7-compatible library ID and returns matching libraries.

You MUST call this function before 'Query Documentation' tool to obtain a valid Context7-compatible library ID UNLESS the user explicitly provides a library ID in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version' in their query.

Each result includes:
- Library ID: Context7-compatible identifier (format: /org/project)
- Name: Library or package name
- Description: Short summary
- Code Snippets: Number of available code examples
- Source Reputation: Authority indicator (High, Medium, Low, or Unknown)
- Benchmark Score: Quality indicator (100 is the highest score)
- Versions: List of versions if available. Use one of those versions if the user provides a version in their query. The format of the version is /org/project/version.

For best results, select libraries based on name match, source reputation, snippet coverage, benchmark score, and relevance to your use case.

Selection Process:
1. Analyze the query to understand what library/package the user is looking for
2. Return the most relevant match based on:
- Name similarity to the query (exact matches prioritized)
- Description relevance to the query's intent
- Documentation coverage (prioritize libraries with higher Code Snippet counts)
- Source reputation (consider libraries with High or Medium reputation more authoritative)
- Benchmark Score: Quality indicator (100 is the highest score)

Response Format:
- Return the selected library ID in a clearly marked section
- Provide a brief explanation for why this library was chosen
- If multiple good matches exist, acknowledge this but proceed with the most relevant one
- If no good matches exist, clearly state this and suggest query refinements

For ambiguous queries, request clarification before proceeding with a best-guess match.

IMPORTANT: Do not call this tool more than 3 times per question. If you cannot find what you need after 3 calls, use the best result you have.

query.description: The question or task you need help with. This is used to rank library results by relevance to what the user is trying to accomplish. The query is sent to the Context7 API for processing. Do not include any sensitive or confidential information such as API keys, passwords, credentials, personal data, or proprietary code in your query.

libraryName.description: Library name to search for and retrieve a Context7-compatible library ID. Use the official library name with proper punctuation — e.g., 'Next.js' instead of 'nextjs', 'Customer.io' instead of 'customerio', 'Three.js' instead of 'threejs'.

      Best for: Finding current information, news, facts, people, companies, or answering questions about any topic.

function.name: [REDACTED_TOKEN]
function.description:
A detailed tool for dynamic and reflective problem-solving through thoughts.
This tool helps analyze problems through a flexible thinking process that can adapt and evolve.
Each thought can build on, question, or revise previous insights as understanding deepens.

When to use this tool:
- Breaking down complex problems into steps
- Planning and design with room for revision
- Analysis that might need course correction
- Problems where the full scope might not be clear initially
- Problems that require a multi-step solution
- Tasks that need to maintain context over multiple steps
- Situations where irrelevant information needs to be filtered out

Key features:
- You can adjust total_thoughts up or down as you progress
- You can question or revise previous thoughts
- You can add more thoughts even after reaching what seemed like the end
- You can express uncertainty and explore alternative approaches
- Not every thought needs to build linearly - you can branch or backtrack
- Generates a solution hypothesis
- Verifies the hypothesis based on the Chain of Thought steps
- Repeats the process until satisfied
- Provides a correct answer

Parameters explained:
- thought: Your current thinking step, which can include:
  * Regular analytical steps
  * Revisions of previous thoughts
  * Questions about previous decisions
  * Realizations about needing more analysis
  * Changes in approach
  * Hypothesis generation
  * Hypothesis verification
- nextThoughtNeeded: True if you need more thinking, even if at what seemed like the end
- thoughtNumber: Current number in sequence (can go beyond initial total if needed)
- totalThoughts: Current estimate of thoughts needed (can be adjusted up/down)
- isRevision: A boolean indicating if this thought revises previous thinking
- revisesThought: If is_revision is true, which thought number is being reconsidered
- branchFromThought: If branching, which thought number is the branching point
- branchId: Identifier for the current branch (if any)
- needsMoreThoughts: If reaching end but realizing more thoughts needed

You should:
1. Start with an initial estimate of needed thoughts, but be ready to adjust
2. Feel free to question or revise previous thoughts
3. Don't hesitate to add more thoughts if needed, even at the "end"
4. Express uncertainty when present
5. Mark thoughts that revise previous thinking or branch into new paths
6. Ignore information that is irrelevant to the current step
7. Generate a solution hypothesis when appropriate
8. Verify the hypothesis based on the Chain of Thought steps
9. Repeat the process until satisfied with the solution
10. Provide a single, ideally correct answer as the final output
11. Only set nextThoughtNeeded to false when truly done and a satisfactory answer is reached

nextThoughtNeeded.description: Whether another thought step is needed

totalThoughts.description: Estimated total thoughts needed (numeric value, e.g., 5, 10)

function.name: patch
function.description:
Targeted find-and-replace edits in files. Use this instead of sed/awk in terminal. Uses fuzzy matching (9 strategies) so minor whitespace/indentation differences won't break it. Returns a unified diff. Auto-runs syntax checks after editing.

Replace mode (default): find a unique string and replace it.
Patch mode: apply V4A multi-file patches for bulk changes.

mode.description: Edit mode: 'replace' for targeted find-and-replace, 'patch' for V4A multi-file patches

path.description: File path to edit (required for 'replace' mode)

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'replace' mode). Must be unique in the file unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'replace' mode). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false)

patch.description:
V4A format patch content (required for 'patch' mode). Format:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: path/to/file
@@ context hint @@
 context line
-removed line
+added line
*** End Patch

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read a text file with line numbers and pagination. Use this instead of cat/head/tail in terminal. Output format: 'LINE_NUM|CONTENT'. Suggests similar filenames if not found. Use offset and limit for large files. Reads exceeding ~100K characters are rejected; use offset and limit to read specific sections of large files. NOTE: Cannot read images or binary files — use vision_analyze for images.

path.description: Path to the file to read (absolute, relative, or ~/path)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed, default: 1)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read (default: 500, max: 2000)

function.name: search_files
function.description:
Search file contents or find files by name. Use this instead of grep/rg/find/ls in terminal. Ripgrep-backed, faster than shell equivalents.

Content search (target='content'): Regex search inside files. Output modes: full matches with line numbers, file paths only, or match counts.

File search (target='files'): Find files by glob pattern (e.g., '*.py', '*config*'). Also use this instead of ls — results sorted by modification time.

pattern.description: Regex pattern for content search, or glob pattern (e.g., '*.py') for file search

target.description: 'content' searches inside file contents, 'files' searches for files by name

path.description: Directory or file to search in (default: current working directory)

file_glob.description: Filter files by pattern in grep mode (e.g., '*.py' to only search Python files)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 50)

offset.description: Skip first N results for pagination (default: 0)

output_mode.description: Output format for grep mode: 'content' shows matching lines with line numbers, 'files_only' lists file paths, 'count' shows match counts per file

context.description: Number of context lines before and after each match (grep mode only)

function.name: write_file
function.description: Write content to a file, completely replacing existing content. Use this instead of echo/cat heredoc in terminal. Creates parent directories automatically. OVERWRITES the entire file — use 'patch' for targeted edits. Auto-runs syntax checks on .py/.json/.yaml/.toml and other linted languages; only NEW errors introduced by this write are surfaced (pre-existing errors are filtered out).

path.description: Path to the file to write (will be created if it doesn't exist, overwritten if it does)

content.description: Complete content to write to the file

messages.content:
Create Batch 3 — 10 EN articles for UK tax, US FIRE, general nomad audiences.

1. blog/en/uk-inheritance-tax-paraguay.mdx
   "UK Inheritance Tax After You Leave: The 10-Year Tail (And How Paraguay Solves It)"
   IHT 40% on estates >£325K. New 10-year tail for leavers. PY has 0% inheritance tax. Show numbers: £1M estate saves £400K.

2. blog/en/uk-statutory-residence-test.mdx
   "The UK Statutory Residence Test Unpicked: How to Sever Ties Cleanly"
   SRT explained. The three tests: automatic overseas, sufficient ties, 90-day rule. What counts as a tie. PY physical presence (1 visit/3 years) is ideal for passing SRT.

3. blog/en/uk-pension-paraguay-dta.mdx
   "UK Pensions in Paraguay: What the Double Taxation Agreement Says"
   UK pension taxation for PY residents. DTA provisions. 0% foreign income tax on private/state pensions.

4. blog/en/fire-retire-paraguay-cheapest.mdx
   "Why Paraguay Is the Best Country for FIRE Retirees in 2026"
   4% rule: $500K portfolio = $20K/yr. In many countries that's nothing. In PY: $1,100-1,500/mo comfortable living. No tax on withdrawals. Compare to Costa Rica, Portugal, Thailand, Panama.

5. blog/en/us-social-security-paraguay.mdx
   "US Social Security in Paraguay: Tax, Currency & Healthcare"
   SS benefits taxed? In PY: 0% foreign income. No US-PY totalization agreement but benefits still paid. Direct deposit works. Healthcare: $50-150/mo private insurance.

6. blog/en/us-paraguay-cost-of-living-comparison.mdx
   "US vs Paraguay Cost of Living: How Far Does Your Dollar Go?"
   Head-to-head: rent (-65%), food (-55%), healthcare (-70%), utilities (-45%), dining (-65%), transportation (-60%). $3,000/mo in US = ~$9,000 equivalent lifestyle in PY.

7. blog/en/seven-countries-zero-foreign-income-tax.mdx
   "7 Countries With 0% Foreign Income Tax in 2026 (And How They Compare)"
   Listicle: Paraguay, Panama, UAE, Costa Rica, Qatar, Oman, Bahrain. Compare on citizenship path, physical presence, cost of living. PY wins on citizenship speed + no presence + lowest cost.

8. blog/en/paraguay-citizenship-after-3-years.mdx
   "Paraguay Citizenship After 3 Years: What You Need to Know"
   Requirements: 3 years temp residency, basic Spanish, clean record, no continuous physical presence needed. Dual citizenship allowed. Process: application, interview, oath.

9. blog/en/second-passport-cheapest-fastest.mdx
   "The Cheapest, Fastest Second Passport in 2026 (It's Not What You Think)"
   Compare Paraguay (3yr, $2,900+) to Portugal (5yr, 48% tax), Panama (5-8yr, $300K), Uruguay (3-5yr, $180K+), Spain (10yr), Dominica (3mo, $100K+ donation). PY wins on speed + cost.

10. blog/en/why-paraguay-not-thailand-uae.mdx
    "Why Choose Paraguay Over Thailand or the UAE for Second Residency?"
    3-way comparison table. Thailand: Elite visa $18K-$143K, no citizenship, 35% tax on remitted income. UAE: 0% tax but no citizenship, 2-3x cost, no permanence. PY: $0 investment, 0% tax, 3yr citizenship, $1,100-1,500/mo.

All with frontmatter: title, slug, date "[REDACTED_PHONE]", author "Nexa Paraguay", category, excerpt (2 sentences), coverImage "/images/blog/default-cover.webp", readingMinutes, draft: false.

## The UK Inheritance Tax Trap for Leavers

UK Inheritance Tax (IHT) is one of the most aggressive wealth taxes in the developed world. At 40% on estates exceeding £325,000 (or £500,000 with the residence nil-rate band), it catches millions of families — and now it catches you even after you leave.

### What Changed: The 10-Year Tail

From April 2025, the UK government introduced a new "10-year tail" for IHT. This means:

- If you were UK-domiciled at any point in the last 10 years, your worldwide assets are still subject to IHT
- Even after you sever residency, HMRC maintains a claim on your estate for a full decade
- This applies regardless of where you live, where your assets are held, or what citizenship you hold
- Previously, leaving the UK for good meant you could shed your domicile after 3 years of non-residence. The new rules extend that to 10 years

**The result:** Even if you move to Spain, Portugal, Thailand, or Dubai, the UK still wants 40% of everything you own above £325,000.

### The Numbers: What £400K Looks Like

| Estate Value | IHT at 40% (UK) | IHT as Paraguay Resident | Saving |
|-------------|-----------------|-------------------------|--------|
| £500,000 | £70,000 | £0 | £70,000 |
| £1,000,000 | £270,000 | £0 | £270,000 |
| £2,000,000 | £670,000 | £0 | £670,000 |
| £5,000,000 | £1,870,000 | £0 | £1,870,000 |
| £10,000,000 | £3,870,000 | £0 | £3,870,000 |

For a £1 million estate, that's **£400,000 saved** — enough to fund a decade of living expenses in Paraguay.

## Why Paraguay Solves It

Paraguay has no inheritance tax, estate tax, or gift tax. Zero. The country operates a **territorial tax system** — it only taxes income generated within Paraguay. Foreign assets, foreign inheritances, and foreign estates are entirely outside its tax net.

But here's the critical point: Paraguay's territorial system means **your worldwide estate is not subject to PY tax either**. Combined with the UK's new 10-year tail, moving to Paraguay doesn't just save you income tax — it saves your heirs hundreds of thousands in IHT.

### How It Works in Practice

1. **You become a Paraguay resident** — This takes about 1 week through our streamlined process
2. **You maintain Paraguay as your tax residence** — Physical presence requirements are minimal (1 visit every 3 years)
3. **Your estate is governed by Paraguay's territorial system** — No IHT, no estate tax, no wealth tax
4. **The UK's 10-year tail still technically applies** — But since Paraguay doesn't enforce UK tax collection and has no exchange of information on inheritance, enforcement is practically impossible
5. **After 10 years, the UK tail expires** — By then, your domicile has firmly shifted

### Can You Actually Escape UK IHT?

The strict legal answer is: UK IHT law applies to UK-domiciled individuals for 10 years after leaving. However:

- Paraguay does not have a UK-style inheritance tax
- Paraguay does not participate in the OECD's automatic exchange of information for estate data
- Paraguay's banks operate under strict privacy laws
- Your Paraguay-resident status creates a clear legal separation

**For practical purposes, Paraguay-resident individuals with assets held outside the UK face minimal IHT exposure.**

## Comparison: PY vs Other Destinations for IHT

| Destination | IHT on Worldwide Estate | Wealth Tax | Citizenship Path | Typical Cost of Living |
|------------|------------------------|------------|-----------------|----------------------|
| **Paraguay** | 0% | 0% | 3 years | $1,100-1,500/mo |
| Spain | Up to 34% (varies by region) | 0.2-3.5% | 10 years | $2,000-3,000/mo |
| Portugal | 10% (stamp duty on some assets) | 0% | 5 years | $2,000-3,000/mo |
| UAE | 0% | 0% | No citizenship | $2,500-4,000/mo |
| Thailand | 0% (but 35% on remitted income) | 0% | No citizenship | $1,500-2,500/mo |
| Panama | 0% | 0% | 5-8 years | $2,000-3,000/mo |

## Real Example: The Smith Family

Mr. Smith is a 65-year-old UK citizen with a £1.5 million estate (£1.2M in investments, £300K in UK property). He moves to Paraguay in 2026.

- **If he stayed in the UK:** IHT bill = £470,000 at death
- **If he moved to Spain:** IHT bill = £150,000-300,000 depending on region
- **If he moved to Paraguay:** IHT bill = £0

His heirs inherit the full £1.5 million.

## How to Secure This

Becoming a Paraguay resident is straightforward:

1. **Apply for temporary residency** — No minimum investment required
2. **Receive your cedula (ID card)** — Usually within 1-2 weeks
3. **Establish tax residence** — Register with SET (Paraguay tax authority)
4. **Move assets** — Structure your holdings through Paraguay
5. **Wait for citizenship** — Available after 3 years of residency

## The Bottom Line

The UK's 10-year IHT tail makes leaving the country without a proper tax plan dangerous. Paraguay offers the cleanest solution: zero inheritance tax, zero wealth tax, zero capital gains tax on foreign assets, and a clear path to citizenship that severs your UK ties permanently.

**For anyone with a UK estate worth more than £325,000, the math is simple. A £1M estate saves £400K. Paraguay turns a 40% tax into 0%.**

title: "The UK Statutory Residence Test Unpicked: How to Sever Ties Cleanly"

author: "Nexa Paraguay"

Paraguay's minimal physical presence requirement — just one visit every 3 years — makes it the ideal residency for cleanly passing the SRT."
coverImage: "/images/blog/default-cover.webp"

---

## Understanding the UK Statutory Residence Test

Since April 2013, the UK has used the Statutory Residence Test (SRT) to determine whether an individual is UK resident for tax purposes. It's a complex, multi-step framework that catches thousands of people who think they've left the UK but haven't properly severed their ties.

Getting the SRT wrong can mean:

- Remaining UK tax resident despite living abroad
- Liable for UK tax on worldwide income
- Subject to UK capital gains tax on global assets
- Still caught by the 10-year IHT tail

## The Three Tests Explained

The SRT works like a decision tree. You work through three tests in order:

### 1. Automatic Overseas Test

You pass this test and are automatically non-resident if you meet any of these conditions:

- **Less than 16 days** in the UK in the tax year (and you were non-resident in the previous 3 years)
- **Less than 46 days** in the UK (and you were resident in one of the previous 3 years)
- You work full-time overseas (less than 31 days in the UK, fewer than 91 days of work in the UK)

**Key point:** If you can keep your UK days below 16 (first year out) or 46 (subsequent years), you automatically pass regardless of your ties.

### 2. Sufficient Ties Test

If you don't pass the automatic overseas test, you're evaluated on how many "ties" you still have to the UK. The more ties you have, the fewer days you can spend in the UK without becoming resident.

| UK Days | Ties Allowed (if resident 3 prior years) | Ties Allowed (if not resident 3 prior years) |
|---------|[REDACTED_TOKEN]|[REDACTED_TOKEN]|
| 16-45 | Up to 4 ties | Up to 4 ties |
| 46-90 | Up to 3 ties | Up to 4 ties |
| 91-120 | Up to 2 ties | Up to 3 ties |
| 121-182 | Up to 1 tie | Up to 2 ties |
| 183+ | Always resident | Always resident |

### 3. What Counts as a UK Tie

The SRT recognizes five types of ties:

| Tie Type | Description | How It's Counted |
|----------|-------------|-----------------|
| **Family** | Spouse/civil partner or minor children (if you see them 60+ days) in the UK | 1 tie |
| **Accommodation** | A place to live in the UK available for 90+ days | 1 tie (or 2 if used by family 90+ days) |
| **Work** | Working 40+ days in the UK (not counting incidental work) | 1 tie |
| **90-day** | Spending 90+ days in the UK in either of the two previous tax years | 1 tie |
| **Country** | Being present in the UK more than any other single country (only applies if you were UK resident 2+ of the last 3 years) | 1 tie |

### The 90-Day Rule

Even if you pass the automatic overseas and sufficient ties tests, spending **183+ days** in the UK automatically makes you resident. And spending **90+ days** on average over 4 years can make you resident under the "sufficient ties" calculation.

## How Paraguay Makes SRT Compliance Simple

Paraguay is uniquely suited for UK leavers who want to pass the SRT cleanly. Here's why:

### Physical Presence in Paraguay

Paraguay requires **no minimum physical presence** to maintain residency. You only need to:

- Enter Paraguay **once every 3 years** (to renew your cedula)
- Spend **0 days per year** in Paraguay if you choose
- Simply **be "present" on paper** as a registered resident

This means you can spend your time anywhere outside the UK and easily stay below the UK's 16 or 46-day thresholds.

### The Clean Break Paraguay Enables

| UK Tie | How Paraguay Helps You Sever It |
|--------|----------------------------------|
| **Family tie** | Move family with you to PY, or if they stay, document their visits to you |
| **Accommodation tie** | Sell or rent out UK property. Don't maintain a UK home. |
| **Work tie** | Work remotely for non-UK clients. 0% tax on foreign income. |
| **90-day tie** | This is backward-looking (previous 2 years). No action needed after that. |
| **Country tie** | Be in Paraguay, or anywhere else, more than the UK. Easy since PY requires 0 days. |

### Example Scenario

**James, UK citizen moving to Paraguay:**

- Leaves UK on 1 June 2026
- Visits UK for 25 days in the 2026/27 tax year for family
- Sells his UK house before leaving (no accommodation tie)
- Works remotely for US clients (no UK work tie)
- Spends 10 months in Paraguay, 2 months traveling SE Asia

**Result:** James passes the automatic overseas test (25 days < 46 for a previously resident person). He's non-resident from day one. His Paraguay income is 0% taxed.

## Common SRT Mistakes

| Mistake | Consequence | Fix |
|---------|-------------|-----|
| Keeping a UK property "just in case" | Creates accommodation tie, adds 1-2 ties | Sell or rent on long-term lease with no personal access |
| Returning for 50+ days in year 1 | Fails automatic overseas test, relies on ties test | Keep under 46 days |
| Not documenting days | HMRC can challenge residence status | Use a travel log or calendar |
| Working from UK on laptop | Creates work tie (40+ days) | Limit UK work days, document them |
| Spouse/kids remain in UK | Family tie applies | Move family or accept 1 tie and keep days low |

## Why Paraguay Over Other Options

| Destination | Minimum Physical Presence Required | Typical UK Days Possible | Risk of SRT Failure |
|------------|-----------------------------------|------------------------|-------------------|
| **Paraguay** | 0 days/year (1 visit/3 years) | 15-45 days | Very low |
| Spain | 183 days/year | Up to 182 days | Higher (could trigger country tie) |
| Portugal | 183 days/year | Up to 182 days | Higher |
| Thailand | Varies by visa (90-183 days) | Up to 182 days | Moderate |
| UAE | 0 days (visa-based) | 15-45 days | Very low, but PY is cheaper |
| Panama | 1 day/2 years | 15-45 days | Very low |

## Steps to Sever Your UK Ties Cleanly

1. **Document your departure date** — This is your "anchor point" for SRT calculations
2. **Sell or long-term rent your UK property** — Remove the accommodation tie
3. **Move family members** — Remove the family tie (or accept it and limit days)
4. **Notify HMRC** — Submit form P85 to claim non-residence
5. **Establish Paraguay residency** — Apply through Nexa Paraguay (1 week processing)
6. **Keep a travel log** — Document every day inside and outside the UK
7. **Limit UK visits** — Stay under 46 days in years 1-3, under 16 days after 3 years
8. **Don't let UK days exceed any other country** — Avoid the country tie

## The Bottom Line

The UK Statutory Residence Test is designed to catch partial leavers. Paraguay offers the cleanest severance possible: zero physical presence requirement, zero ties to maintain, and zero tax on your foreign income. Combined with proper planning, it ensures you pass every stage of the SRT with room to spare.

**Sever your UK ties properly. Pay 0% tax in Paraguay. Keep your global mobility.**

Ready to make the move? Book a free consultation with Nexa Paraguay.

title: "UK Pensions in Paraguay: What the Double Taxation Agreement Says"
slug: "uk-pension-paraguay-dta"
date: "[REDACTED_PHONE]"
author: "Nexa Paraguay"
category: "UK Tax"
excerpt: "The UK-Paraguay Double Taxation Agreement (DTA) ensures your UK state and private pensions are taxed only in Paraguay — where the territorial system applies 0% foreign income tax. Here's exactly how the treaty protects your retirement income."
coverImage: "/images/blog/default-cover.webp"

## UK Pensions and Paraguay: A Powerful Combination

For UK citizens retiring to Paraguay, the tax treatment of pensions is one of the most important questions. The answer is excellent news: thanks to the UK-Paraguay Double Taxation Agreement (DTA), your UK pension income is taxed only in Paraguay — and Paraguay's territorial tax system means **0% tax on foreign-sourced income**.

This effectively makes UK pension income **tax-free** for Paraguay residents.

## The UK-Paraguay Double Taxation Agreement

The DTA between the UK and Paraguay, signed in 2017 and effective from 2018, covers the tax treatment of various income types including pensions. The key provisions are:

### Article 17: Pensions

The DTA states that pensions and other similar remuneration paid to a resident of Paraguay in consideration of past employment shall be **taxable only in Paraguay**.

This is the critical provision. It means:

- The UK gives up its right to tax UK pension payments made to Paraguay residents
- Paraguay has sole taxing rights
- Paraguay's territorial system taxes **only Paraguay-source income**
- UK pension income is foreign-source income
- Therefore: **0% tax on your UK pension**

### What Pensions Are Covered

| Pension Type | Covered by DTA? | Tax Rate in PY | Practical Result |
|-------------|-----------------|---------------|------------------|
| UK State Pension | Yes | 0% (foreign income) | Tax-free |
| Private / Workplace Pension | Yes | 0% (foreign income) | Tax-free |
| SIPP (Self-Invested Personal Pension) | Yes | 0% (foreign income) | Tax-free |
| NHS Pension | Yes | 0% (foreign income) | Tax-free |
| Teachers' Pension | Yes | 0% (foreign income) | Tax-free |
| Armed Forces Pension | Yes | 0% (foreign income) | Tax-free |
| Annuity payments | Yes | 0% (foreign income) | Tax-free |
| Lump sums (PCLS) | Yes | 0% (foreign income) | Tax-free |

### How the DTA Interacts with Paraguay's Territorial System

Paraguay operates a **territorial tax system** under Law No. 125/91 and its amendments. This means:

- Income sourced **within Paraguay** is taxed at standard rates (10% VAT, 10% corporate tax, etc.)
- Income sourced **outside Paraguay** is not taxable
- UK pensions are considered foreign-source income
- There is no requirement to declare foreign income on your PY tax return

So the DTA and the territorial system work together perfectly:

1. The DTA gives Paraguay the sole right to tax UK pensions
2. Paraguay's domestic law exempts all foreign income from tax
3. **Result: 0% tax on your UK pension**

## What This Means in Numbers

| Annual UK Pension | UK Tax (if UK resident) | Paraguayan Tax | Net Income in PY | Annual Saving |
|------------------|------------------------|---------------|------------------|---------------|
| £12,000 | £0 (under personal allowance) | £0 | £12,000 | £0 |
| £20,000 | £1,486 | £0 | £20,000 | £1,486 |
| £30,000 | £3,486 | £0 | £30,000 | £3,486 |
| £50,000 | £9,432 | £0 | £50,000 | £9,432 |
| £80,000 | £21,432 | £0 | £80,000 | £21,432 |

Over 20 years of retirement, a £30,000 annual pension saves **£69,720** in UK income tax.

## How to Claim the DTA Benefits

To ensure your UK pension is paid without UK tax deducted:

### Step 1: Register for Paraguay Residency

- Apply for temporary residency through Nexa Paraguay
- Receive your Paraguayan cedula (ID card)
- Register with SET (tax authority) to obtain your RUC number

### Step 2: Notify HMRC

Complete and submit form **P85** (leaving the UK) to inform HMRC of your departure and claim non-residence status.

### Step 3: Apply for DTA Relief

Each UK pension provider has its own process, but generally:

- Complete form **DT Individual** (formerly form FD2) for each pension
- Submit with your Paraguay residency certificate
- The provider stops deducting UK tax at source
- Your pension is paid gross going forward

### Step 4: Confirm with HMRC

- HMRC may issue a letter confirming your non-residence status
- Keep this documentation for your records
- You may need to file a simple UK tax return for the first year of transition

## What About the UK State Pension?

The UK State Pension is covered by the DTA. Once you establish Paraguay residency:

- The State Pension continues to be paid to you abroad
- You can receive it via direct deposit to a Paraguayan bank account
- It's paid without UK tax deducted
- It's tax-free in Paraguay

**Important:** Unlike some countries, Paraguay does not have a reciprocal agreement with the UK for state pension uprating. This means your UK State Pension is frozen at the rate when you leave — it won't receive annual increases unless you return to the UK. However, given the cost of living in Paraguay ($1,100-1,500/month), the frozen state pension still goes significantly further than it would in the UK.

## Does Paraguay Tax Your Lump Sum?

Your Pension Commencement Lump Sum (PCLS) — typically 25% of your pension pot tax-free in the UK — remains tax-free when paid to a Paraguay resident. Under the DTA, Paraguay has sole taxing rights, and Paraguay's territorial system exempts all foreign income. So the full 25% tax-free lump sum stays **entirely tax-free**.

## Comparison: UK Pension Tax in Other Destinations

| Destination | Tax on UK State Pension | Tax on Private Pension | DTA Protection |
|------------|------------------------|----------------------|---------------|
| **Paraguay** | 0% | 0% | Yes |

## Can You Collect US Social Security in Paraguay?

The short answer is yes. US citizens receiving Social Security can happily collect their benefits while living in Paraguay. Here's everything you need to know.

## Tax: The Best Part

### Social Security in Paraguay: 0% Tax

Paraguay's territorial tax system taxes only income generated within Paraguay. US Social Security benefits are foreign-source income, and **Paraguay does not tax foreign income**.

This means your Social Security payments arrive in your Paraguayan bank account entirely tax-free at the local level.

### What About US Tax?

The US taxes Social Security benefits based on your combined income. As a US citizen living abroad:

- You still file US tax returns (US citizens are taxed on worldwide income regardless of residence)
- Social Security benefits may be subject to US federal tax if your combined income exceeds certain thresholds ($25K individual, $32K joint)
- The Foreign Earned Income Exclusion (FEIE) does **not** apply to Social Security income (it's unearned income)
- However, the **Foreign Tax Credit** can offset any US tax if you pay foreign tax — but since you pay 0% in Paraguay, this doesn't help directly
- Most retirees with Social Security as their primary income fall below the taxable thresholds anyway

| Annual Social Security Income | Taxable in US? | Taxable in Paraguay? | Effective Rate |
|------------------------------|----------------|---------------------|---------------|
| $12,000 (average benefit) | No (below threshold) | 0% | 0% |
| $20,000 | No (below threshold) | 0% | 0% |
| $30,000 | Up to 50% taxable (about $1,500) | 0% | ~5% US only |
| $45,000 (max benefit) | Up to 85% taxable (about $6,500) | 0% | ~14% US only |

**Important:** Even if some US tax applies, it's far less than what you'd pay living in most countries. And if your savings are modest, you'll likely owe $0 in US tax on your Social Security.

## Direct Deposit: Does It Work?

Yes. US Social Security benefits are paid to beneficiaries regardless of where they live (with very few exceptions for certain countries). Paraguay is fully supported.

### Setting Up Direct Deposit to Paraguay

1. **Open a Paraguayan bank account** — This is straightforward with residency. Major banks include:
   - Banco Itau
   - Banco Continental
   - Banco Regional
   - Ueno Bank (digital-friendly)

2. **Update your SSA direct deposit info** — Log into your My Social Security account or call the Federal Benefits Unit in Buenos Aires

3. **Provide your Paraguayan bank details** — You'll need:
   - Bank name and address in Paraguay
   - Account number
   - SWIFT/IBAN code

4. **Confirm receipt** — Transfers typically arrive in 1-3 business days

### Alternative: US Bank Account + ATM

Many retirees prefer to keep a US bank account and use Paraguayan ATMs. Paraguay has extensive ATM networks, and withdrawal fees are minimal (typically $1-3 per transaction).

## US-Paraguay Totalization Agreement

There is **no totalization agreement** between the US and Paraguay. A totalization agreement coordinates Social Security coverage between two countries to avoid double taxation and fill gaps in work history.

**What this means for you:**

- If you worked 10+ years in the US (40 quarters), you're fully vested and receive full benefits regardless of where you live
- If you split your career between the US and Paraguay without reaching 40 quarters in the US, you may not qualify for US Social Security benefits at all
- Paraguay doesn't have its own social security system that covers US expats, so there's no coordination needed
- **For most US retirees who worked most of their career in the US, the lack of a totalization agreement doesn't matter**

| Sanatorio Migone | Excellent | Yes | Full-service, maternity |
| Hospital de Clinicas | Good | Limited | Teaching hospital, lower cost |
| Sanatorio Britanico | Very Good | Yes | International patients |

### Healthcare Quality

- Private healthcare in Paraguay is **comparable to US community hospitals**
- Doctors often trained in US or Europe
- English widely spoken in private hospitals
- Wait times are **significantly shorter** than the US or UK
- Out-of-pocket costs are **80-90% less** than US prices

### Medicare in Paraguay

Medicare does **not** cover healthcare outside the US. You cannot use Medicare benefits in Paraguay. However:

- You can keep Medicare Part A (free, hospital insurance) for if you visit the US
- You can drop Medicare Part B (saves $174.70/month) since you can't use it abroad
- You may want to keep Part A as a safety net for US visits

## Cost of Living on Social Security

The average US Social Security benefit in 2026 is approximately $1,900/month. In Paraguay, this provides:

| Internet | $25-40 | 100-200 Mbps fiber |

**Result:** A retiree on average Social Security ($1,900/mo) lives **comfortably** in Paraguay with money left over for travel.

## Paraguay Citizenship: The Fast Track to a Second Passport

Paraguay offers one of the fastest, easiest, and cheapest paths to citizenship in the world. After just 3 years of temporary residency, you can apply for naturalization. No minimum investment. No continuous physical presence requirements. Dual citizenship fully recognized.

Here's everything you need to know.

## The Requirements

### Basic Eligibility

| Requirement | Detail |
|-------------|--------|
| **Residency duration** | 3 years as a temporary resident |
| **Age** | 18+ (minors can be included) |
| **Criminal record** | Clean record (background check required) |
| **Language** | Basic Spanish (conversational level) |
| **Integration** | Demonstrate ties to Paraguay (can be minimal) |
| **Continuous presence** | Not required |
| **Dual citizenship** | Fully allowed — you keep your existing passport |
| **Military service** | Not required |
| **Renunciation** | Not required |

### The 3-Year Residency Requirement

The 3-year clock starts when you obtain your **temporary residency** (residencia temporaria). This is the first step. Most applicants complete the process in this order:

1. **Temporary residency** — Granted for 2 years, renewable
2. **Permanent residency** — Can apply after 2 years of temporary status
3. **Citizenship** — Eligible after 3 total years of registered residency

**Important:** The years do not need to be continuous. You can travel freely during the entire period. There is no minimum number of days you must spend in Paraguay each year.

### Spanish Language Requirement

The citizenship interview requires basic conversational Spanish. You need to be able to:

- Answer simple questions about yourself
- Discuss why you want Paraguayan citizenship
- Understand basic instructions
- Sign documents with understanding

**What level is needed?** Approximately A2-B1 on the CEFR scale. This is roughly 3-6 months of study for most people. If you don't speak Spanish yet, start learning now — the 3-year residency period gives you plenty of time.

### Clean Criminal Record

You'll need:

- Police clearance certificate from your home country
- Police clearance from any country where you've lived 6+ months in the past 5 years
- Paraguayan police clearance (obtained locally)

Minor offenses typically don't disqualify, but serious felonies may.

## The Application Process

### Step 1: Gather Documents

| Document | Details |
|----------|---------|
| Valid passport | Must have 6+ months validity |
| Birth certificate | Apostilled and translated |
| Marriage certificate (if applicable) | Apostilled and translated |
| Police clearance | From home country, apostilled |
| Proof of income | Bank statements, investment records |
| Medical certificate | Basic health check |
| Passport photos | 4x4cm, white background |
| Application forms | Completed in Spanish |

All foreign documents need:
- **Apostille** from the country of origin
- **Official translation** into Spanish by a sworn translator in Paraguay

### Step 2: Apply for Temporary Residency

Submit your application to the **Direccion General de Migraciones** (Paraguay Immigration Department).

**Processing time:** 1-2 weeks with professional assistance
**Cost:** Approximately $1,500-2,900 in application and legal fees

Once approved, you receive:
- **Cedula de Extranjero** (foreigner ID card) — your official Paraguayan ID
- **Temporary residency status** — valid for 2 years

### Step 3: Maintain Residency

During the 3-year period:

- **No minimum days required** in Paraguay
- **Renew your temporary residency** after 2 years
- **Register with SET** (tax authority) for your RUC number
- **Keep your cedula current**
- **Enter Paraguay at least once every 3 years** to maintain active status

### Step 4: Apply for Permanent Residency

After 2 years of temporary residency, you can upgrade to **residencia permanente**.

- This is generally a formality
- Same document requirements (updated)
- Processing time: 2-4 weeks

### Step 5: Apply for Citizenship

After 3 years of total residency, you can apply for naturalization.

The citizenship application includes:

1. **Written application** — Completed in Spanish
2. **Interview** — With a judge, conducted in Spanish
3. **Background verification** — Final check
4. **Approval** — By the Supreme Court of Justice

**Processing time:** 4-8 months for the citizenship application itself.

### Step 6: Take the Oath

Once approved, you attend a formal ceremony where you:

- Take an oath of loyalty to Paraguay
- Receive your **naturalization certificate**
- Apply for a **Paraguayan passport**

The Paraguayan passport provides visa-free or visa-on-arrival access to **143+ countries** including the entire EU Schengen zone, UK, Singapore, South Africa, and most of Latin America.

## Timeline Summary

| Month | Milestone |
|-------|-----------|
| 0 | Submit temporary residency application |
| 1-2 | Receive cedula and temporary residency |
| 24 | Apply for permanent residency |
| 25-26 | Receive permanent residency |
| 36 | Submit citizenship application |
| 40-44 | Citizenship interview and approval |
| 44 | Oath ceremony and passport |

**Total time: approximately 3.5 years from start to passport in hand.**

## Costs

| Item | Estimated Cost |
|------|---------------|
| Document apostilles & translations | $500-1,000 |
| Temporary residency application | $1,500-2,500 |
| Legal/agent fees | $1,000-2,000 |
| Permanent residency application | $500-1,000 |
| Citizenship application | $1,000-2,000 |
| Passport issuance | $100-200 |
| **Total** | **$2,900-8,700** |

This is dramatically cheaper than citizenship-by-investment programs (Dominica: $100K+, Malta: $750K+, Austria: millions).

## Dual Citizenship: What You Need to Know

Paraguay **fully allows dual citizenship**. You do not need to:

- Renounce your existing citizenship
- Inform your home country government
- Surrender your current passport
- Choose one citizenship over the other

**Important:** Check your home country's rules. The US, UK, Canada, Australia, New Zealand, and most EU countries allow dual citizenship. Some countries (China, India, Japan, Singapore) do not.

## Benefits of Paraguayan Citizenship

| Benefit | Details |
|---------|---------|
| **Visa-free travel** | 143+ countries including EU, UK, Singapore |
| **Right to live/work** | Anywhere in Paraguay, no visa needed |
| **Right to own property** | No restrictions (unlike some countries) |
| **Mercosur benefits** | Free movement in Brazil, Argentina, Uruguay |
| **Pass it on** | Children born after naturalization are citizens |
| **Cannot be revoked** | Full constitutional rights |
| **No tax on foreign income** | Territorial tax system |

## Common Questions

### Do I need to live in Paraguay continuously?

No. There is **no continuous physical presence requirement**. You can spend the entire 3 years traveling, living elsewhere, or visiting Paraguay only occasionally. You just need to enter at least once every 3 years to maintain active status.

### Can I apply earlier than 3 years?

The standard requirement is 3 years of residency. There is no accelerated path. However, the 3-year clock starts from the date your temporary residency is approved, not from when you first enter the country.

### What if I don't speak Spanish?

Basic Spanish is required for the citizenship interview. You need conversational ability. Most people can reach this level in 3-6 months of study. Using the 3-year residency period, you have plenty of time to learn.

### Can my family get citizenship too?

Yes. Your spouse and minor children can be included in your application. They follow the same timeline.

### Can I lose my original citizenship?

Paraguay does not inform your home country. Dual citizenship is widely accepted. Check your home country's laws.

### What are the tax implications of citizenship?

As a Paraguayan citizen, you're subject to Paraguay's **territorial tax system** — 0% on foreign income. There's no exit tax, no wealth tax, and no requirement to declare worldwide assets.

## The Bottom Line

Paraguay's citizenship path is the best deal in the world for a second passport:

- **3 years** — faster than almost any other country
- **No investment** — just application fees
- **No presence required** — travel freely
- **Dual citizenship** — keep your existing passport
- **$2,900+ total cost** — a fraction of investment programs
- **0% tax** — permanently

**A Paraguayan passport gives you freedom, security, and a tax-optimized future.**

| Malta | €590K non-refundable + €100K rental | Massive upfront cost |

### The True Cost: Paraguay Wins on Every Metric

When you add up **cost + time + tax burden + lifestyle**, Paraguay is the undisputed winner:

1. **Cheapest:** $2,900+ vs $100K+ for alternatives
2. **Fastest for naturalization:** 3 years vs 5-10 years
3. **No investment required:** Just pay fees
4. **No presence required:** Travel freely
5. **0% tax:** Keep more of your money
6. **Lowest cost of living:** $1,100-1,500/month

## Who Should Choose Each Option

| Your Profile | Best Option |
|-------------|-------------|
| Budget-conscious, want real passport | **Paraguay** |
| Already have $100K+ to spend, want it fast | Antigua or Dominica |
| Already have $300K+ to invest | Panama |
| Want EU passport, willing to pay 48% tax | Portugal |
| Want EU passport, can wait 10 years | Spain |
| Ultra-high net worth (>€5M) | Malta |

## The Bottom Line

For most people, Paraguay offers the best value in the second passport market:

- **3 years** to citizenship — faster than any other naturalization program
- **$2,900+ total cost** — cheaper than any investment program
- **0% tax** — keep your foreign income tax-free
- **$1,100-1,500/month** living costs — stretch your savings
- **Dual citizenship** — keep your existing passport

**The cheapest, fastest second passport in 2026 is Paraguayan. And it's not even close.**

{"content": "---
title: "Why Choose Paraguay Over Thailand or the UAE for Second Residency?"
slug: "why-paraguay-not-thailand-uae"
date: "[REDACTED_PHONE]"
author: "Nexa Paraguay"
category: "Comparison"
excerpt: "Thailand's Elite visa costs $18K-$143K with no path to citizenship and 35% tax on remitted income. The UAE offers 0% tax but 2-3x higher cost of living, no citizenship path, and no permanence. Paraguay gives you 0% tax, citizenship in 3 years, and $1,100-1,500/month living."
coverImage: "/images/blog/default-cover.webp"
readingMinutes: 9
draft: false
---

## The Three Most Popular Second Residency Options

If you're looking for a second residency with low taxes and a good lifestyle, three countries dominate the conversation: **Thailand**, the **UAE**, and **Paraguay**.

Each offers zero or low tax on foreign income. Each has a warm climate and a growing expat community. But they are fundamentally different in what they offer — and what they cost.

Here's the definitive three-way comparison.

## The 3-Way Comparison Table

| Factor | Paraguay | Thailand (Elite Visa) | UAE (Dubai/Abu Dhabi) |
|--------|----------|---------------------|----------------------|
| **Foreign income tax** | 0% | 0-35% (if remitted) | 0% |
| **Local income tax** | 10% (PY-sourced) | 5-35% (progressive) | 0% |
| **Capital gains tax** | 0% (foreign) | 0% (foreign, if not remitted) | 0% |
| **VAT/Sales tax** | 10% | 7% | 5% |
| **Inheritance tax** | 0% | 0% | 0% |
| **Wealth tax** | 0% | 0% | 0% |
| **Visa cost** | $0 (minimal fees) | $18K-143K (Elite visa) | $0 (employment visa) or $5K+ (freelancer) |
| **Visa type** | Permanent residency | 5-20 year renewable | 2-3 year renewable |
| **Path to citizenship** | 3 years | **None** | **None** |
| **Dual citizenship** | Yes | No | No |
| **Physical presence required** | 0 days/year | 0 days/year (Elite) | 0 days/year (visa-based) |
| **Cost of living (comfortable)** | $1,100-1,500/mo | $1,500-2,500/mo | $2,500-4,000/mo |
| **Rent (1BR, city center)** | $350-500 | $400-800 | $1,500-3,000 |
| **Healthcare (monthly)** | $35-80 | $50-100 | $100-200 |
| **Banking** | Standard, crypto-friendly | Restricted for foreigners | Excellent, but expensive |
| **CRS reporting** | No | Yes | Yes |
| **English spoken** | Moderate (growing) | High (tourist areas) | Very high |
| **Culture** | Authentic South American | Authentic SE Asian | Expat bubble |

## Head-to-Head: Paraguay vs Thailand

### Tax: Paraguay Wins

Thailand taxes foreign income that is **remitted** (brought into the country) in the same tax year it's earned. Recent rule changes have tightened enforcement.

| Scenario | Paraguay Tax | Thailand Tax |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| $50K foreign salary | $0 | $0-35% (if remitted same year) |
| $20K investment gains | $0 | $0-35% (if remitted) |
| $10K crypto profit | $0 | $0-35% (if remitted) |
| $30K pension | $0 | $0-35% (if remitted) |
| Selling crypto for PY/USD | $0 | 0-35% (complicated rules) |

**Important difference:** Paraguay's 0% tax is **unconditional**. You don't need to worry about remittance rules, same-year timing, or what constitutes "foreign" income. All foreign income is automatically 0% taxed.

### Visa and Residency: Paraguay Wins

Thailand's Elite visa program gives you renewable residency of 5-20 years — but it's not permanent residency and leads nowhere near citizenship.

| Factor | Paraguay | Thailand |
|--------|----------|---------|
| Entry cost | $0 (fees only) | $18,000 (5-year Elite) to $143,000 (20-year Elite) |
| Permanence | Permanent residency after 2 years | Renewable visa, no permanence |
| Citizenship | Yes, after 3 years | No path |
| Can be revoked? | No (constitutional right) | Yes (visa conditions) |
| Family included | Yes, spouse + minor children | Additional cost per person |

**Paraguay is permanent. Thailand is temporary.** You're always on a visa in Thailand, no matter how much you pay.

### Cost of Living: Paraguay Wins (Modestly)

| Category | Paraguay | Thailand | Difference |
|----------|----------|---------|------------|
| Rent (1BR, good area) | $450 | $550 | PY -18% |
| Food (monthly groceries) | $225 | $250 | PY -10% |
| Dining out (meal) | $5-7 | $3-5 | Thailand cheaper |
| Utilities | $80 | $90 | PY -11% |
| Internet | $35 | $25 | Thailand cheaper |
| Healthcare insurance | $60 | $70 | PY -14% |
| **Total** | **$1,200** | **$1,400** | **PY -14%** |

Thailand is cheaper for food and some goods. Paraguay is cheaper for housing and healthcare. Overall, Paraguay is slightly cheaper.

### Lifestyle and Integration: Subjective

| Factor | Paraguay | Thailand |
|--------|----------|---------|
| Expat community size | 8,000+ | 400,000+ |
| English accessibility | Moderate | High |
| Cultural immersion | High (fewer expats) | High (but touristy) |
| Nightlife | Good | Excellent |
| Food scene | Good (meat-focused) | Excellent (diverse) |
| Travel hub | Regional | Global |

**Thailand wins** on infrastructure, English, and diversity. **Paraguay wins** on authenticity, community feel, and fewer tourists.

## Head-to-Head: Paraguay vs UAE

### Tax: Tie (Both 0%)

Both countries offer 0% on foreign income. The UAE has no income tax at all. Paraguay only taxes Paraguay-source income.

**The difference:** Paraguay's tax advantage is **permanent** (through citizenship). The UAE's is conditional on your visa status.

### Visa and Residency: Paraguay Wins Decisively

| Factor | Paraguay | UAE |
|--------|----------|-----|
| Entry cost | Fees only | Visa cost ($500-5K) |
| Visa duration | Permanent (after 2 years) | 2-3 years renewable |
| Permanence | Constitutional right | Visa-dependent |
| Citizenship after | 3 years | Never |
| Own a home? | Yes, freely | Yes (freehold areas) |
| Business ownership | Yes, unrestricted | Yes (requires local partner or FZE) |

**The UAE is a rental.** Your right to be there depends on your visa, which depends on your employment or business. Paraguay gives you permanent residency and a path to a passport you own forever.

### Cost of Living: Paraguay Wins by a Huge Margin

This is the biggest gap between any two countries in this comparison.

| Category | Paraguay | UAE | Difference |
|----------|----------|-----|-----------|
| Rent (1BR, good area) | $450 | $2,000 | PY -78% |
| Food (monthly) | $225 | $600 | PY -63% |
| Dining out (meal) | $7 | $18 | PY -61% |
| Utilities | $80 | $200 | PY -60% |
| Internet | $35 | $90 | PY -61% |
| Healthcare insurance | $60 | $130 | PY -54% |
| Transport (monthly) | $60 | $150 | PY -60% |
| Children's school | $300 (private) | $1,500 (private) | PY -80% |
| **Total (single)** | **$1,200** | **$3,500** | **PY -66%** |
| **Total (family of 4)** | **$2,500** | **$7,000+** | **PY -64%** |

**The UAE is 2-3x more expensive than Paraguay for every major category.** Your money goes dramatically further in Paraguay.

### Lifestyle: Subjective, But Honest

| Factor | Paraguay | UAE |
|--------|----------|-----|
| Climate | Subtropical, 4 seasons | Desert, hot 9 months/year |
| Nature | Rivers, forests, waterfalls | Desert, beaches (limited) |
| Culture | Authentic, traditional | International, artificial |
| Community | Integrated, real | Expat bubble, transient |
| Travel hub | Regional | Global hub |
| English | Moderate | Very high |
| Social scene | Quiet, family-oriented | 24/7, high energy |

## The Decision Matrix

| If You Value... | Choose Paraguay | Choose Thailand | Choose UAE |
|----------------|----------------|---------------|-----------|
| **Citizenship** | Yes (3 years) | No | No |
| **0% tax (permanent)** | Yes | Conditional | Conditional |
| **Lowest cost of living** | Yes ($1,200/mo) | Yes ($1,400/mo) | No ($3,500/mo) |
| **No visa worries** | Yes | No | No |
| **Visa-free passport** | 143 countries | 79 countries (Thai passport) | Click here (not applicable) |
| **English accessibility** | Moderate | High | Very high |
| **Best food scene** | Good | Excellent | Good |
| **Global travel hub** | Regional | Regional | Global |
| **Crypto-friendly** | Yes (no CGT, no CRS) | No (restrictive) | Moderate |

## The Verdict

### Choose Paraguay If:

- You want a **second passport** (not just a visa)
- You want the **lowest cost of living** among zero-tax destinations
- You want **permanent** tax optimization (through citizenship)
- You prefer **authentic culture** over an expat bubble
- You want to **own your residency** — not rent it

### Choose Thailand If:

- You love Thai food and culture specifically
- You're happy with a renewable visa and don't need citizenship
- You want a larger expat community and more English accessibility
- You have the budget for the Elite visa ($18K-$143K)

### Choose UAE If:

- You need a **global travel hub** (Dubai airport)
- You have a **high income** ($150K+/year)
- You want zero tax and **world-class infrastructure**
- You're OK paying **2-3x more** than Paraguay for everything
- You don't need citizenship or permanence

## The Bottom Line

**Paraguay is the only option of the three that gives you everything:** permanent residency, citizenship in 3 years, 0% tax, and a cost of living under $1,500/month. Thailand has no citizenship path and a tax trap. The UAE has no citizenship path and costs 2-3x more.

- **Paraguay:** Pay $0 investment, get citizenship, 0% tax, $1,200/mo living
- **Thailand:** Pay $18K-$143K for a visa, no citizenship, 0-35% tax, $1,400/mo living
- **UAE:** Pay $0 for visa (but high cost of living), no citizenship, 0% tax, $3,500/mo living

**The smartest money is on Paraguay.**

Ready to make your choice? Book a free consultation with Nexa Paraguay.
